priority1.title: Self-Healing Concrete with Bacteria
priority1.description: Bio-concrete that repairs its own cracks using limestone-producing bacteria, extending structure life in humid tropical conditions.

priority1.title: Transparent Wood Insulation
priority1.description: Engineered wood that provides thermal insulation while allowing light transmission. Perfect for tropical daylighting without heat gain.

priority1.quote: Architecture must learn from the vernacular, not impose upon it.
priority1.author: Hassan Fathy
priority1.context: Egyptian architect, pioneer of sustainable vernacular architecture

priority1.title: Mycelium-Based Insulation Panels
priority1.description: Grown from mushroom roots, these panels offer excellent thermal and acoustic properties with zero carbon footprint.

priority1.quote: The tropical house must breathe. It must respond to sun, wind, and rain.
priority1.author: Geoffrey Bawa
priority1.context: Sri Lankan architect, father of Tropical Modernism

priority1.title: Phase-Change Material Bricks
priority1.description: Bricks infused with PCM that absorb heat during day and release it at night, naturally regulating indoor temperatures.

priority2.title: Passive Cooling Reduces Energy Use by 60% in Singapore Study
priority2.description: New research shows traditional ventilation techniques combined with modern design can dramatically reduce cooling loads in tropical high-rises.

priority2.title: Bamboo Housing Project Wins UN Sustainability Award
priority2.description: Affordable housing development in Indonesia uses locally-sourced bamboo and traditional joinery techniques.

priority2.title: New Guidelines for Tropical Green Building Certification
priority2.description: Updated standards now prioritize natural ventilation and local materials over energy-intensive cooling systems.

priority3.title: AI Optimizes Building Orientation for Tropical Climates
priority3.description: Machine learning algorithm analyzes sun paths, wind patterns, and topography to suggest optimal building placement.

priority3.title: Generative Design Creates Climate-Responsive Facades
priority3.description: New software generates facade patterns that maximize shade while maintaining views and natural light.

priority3.title: Neural Networks Predict Thermal Comfort in Real-Time
priority3.description: AI system monitors indoor conditions and adjusts passive ventilation systems automatically.

quotes.quote: Architecture must learn from the vernacular, not impose upon it.
quotes.author: Hassan Fathy
quotes.context: Egyptian architect, pioneer of sustainable vernacular architecture

quotes.quote: The tropical house must breathe. It must respond to sun, wind, and rain.
quotes.author: Geoffrey Bawa
quotes.context: Sri Lankan architect, father of Tropical Modernism

quotes.quote: We must design with climate, not against it.
quotes.author: Victor Olgyay
quotes.context: Author of 'Design with Climate', bioclimatic architecture pioneer

quotes.quote: Traditional architecture is sustainable by default. We must rediscover what was never lost.
quotes.author: Ralph Erskine

quotes.quote: In the tropics, the wall is not the boundary. Space flows, air circulates.
quotes.author: Lloyd Wright

quotes.quote: Bamboo is the steel of the tropics. It grows fast, it's strong, and it's ours.
quotes.author: Vo Trong Nghia

quotes.quote: Passive design is not a technology. It's an attitude.
quotes.author: Glenn Murcutt

quotes.quote: The best building in the tropics is one that needs no air conditioning.
quotes.author: Kerry Hill

materials.title: Recycled Plastic Lumber
materials.description: Weather-resistant lumber made from ocean plastics. Ideal for tropical outdoor structures, resistant to rot and insects.
